adjectives.example: My dad has a big car that fits our whole family.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Big means large, not small.

adjectives.example: I found a small ladybug on the green leaf.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Small means little, not big.

adjectives.example: I feel happy when I play with my friends at the park.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Happy is when you feel good and smile.

adjectives.example: I feel sad when my favorite toy breaks.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Sad is when you feel like crying.

adjectives.example: The soup is hot, so I blow on it before I eat.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Hot means very warm, like fire or the sun.

adjectives.example: The ice cream is cold and delicious on a summer day.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Cold means not warm, like ice.

adjectives.example: The cheetah is fast and can run faster than a car.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Fast means moving quickly, not slow.

adjectives.example: The turtle is slow but very cute.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Slow means not fast.

adjectives.example: My blanket is soft and keeps me warm at night.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Soft means nice and squishy, like a pillow.

adjectives.example: The rock is hard and I cannot break it.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Hard means not soft, like a table.

adjectives.example: The fire truck makes a loud siren that wakes me up.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Loud means noisy, like a drum.

adjectives.example: The library is quiet so people can read.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Quiet means not noisy, like a whisper.

adjectives.meaning: Giving out a lot of light
adjectives.example: The flashlight is bright in the dark room.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Bright means shining a lot of light.

adjectives.example: It is dark outside when I go to bed.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Dark means no light, like night time.

adjectives.meaning: Not old, recently made or bought
adjectives.example: I have new shoes that light up when I walk.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: New means fresh and never used before.

adjectives.meaning: Having existed for a long time
adjectives.example: My grandma has an old photo from when she was a kid.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Old means something that has been here a long time.

adjectives.example: I put on clean clothes after my bath.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Clean means not dirty.

adjectives.meaning: Not clean, with dirt or stains
adjectives.example: My hands are dirty after playing in the mud.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Dirty means messy or not clean.

adjectives.example: Strawberries are sweet and yummy.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Sweet means tasting like candy.

adjectives.example: Lemons are sour and make my face scrunch up.
adjectives.kid_friendly_explanation: Sour means a sharp taste, like a lemon.
